mnemonics.title: Capacity Planning Strategies
mnemonics.content: LLM - Lag, Lead, Match: LAG adds after, LEAD adds before, MATCH adds incrementally - think of 'matching' demand step by step

mnemonics.title: Performance Metrics
mnemonics.content: C.R.T.S.W - CPU, Response, Throughput, Wait time, Service time - the 'CRT' shows performance clearly, 'SW' shows what's slow

acronyms.usage: The primary processor that executes instructions - key resource in capacity planning
Measure of throughput for transaction processing systems
衡量容量规划投资的回报率
Defines acceptable performance levels that capacity must achieve
Set of techniques to manage resources and guarantee performance
Memory management technique using disk to extend RAM capacity

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a funnel: wide top (input), narrow bottom (bottleneck). No matter how much you pour (resources), output limited by narrow part. System throughput limited by most constrained resource.
Think of fuel gauge: 0-20% (waste), 20-80% (optimal), 80-100% (danger). Like a car, best performance in 'sweet spot' - not too empty, not running on fumes.
People at checkout counters: more counters (servers) = shorter lines. Utilization ρ = customers arriving / customers served. If ρ≥1, queue grows forever!

analogies.analogy: Capacity is like the number of seats in a theater (how many can watch now). Scalability is like the ability to add more seats when needed (growing to accommodate more).
Like a restaurant overstaffing before a known busy season - prevents customer disappointment but may have idle workers initially.
A four-lane highway narrowing to one lane - traffic flow limited by the narrow section regardless of how many cars enter.
Like using a storage unit for items that don't fit in your apartment - extends space but access is slower (like going to storage vs reaching into your closet).
